title: Polvin Kut

Yaddle appears as a member of the Jedi High Council during [[Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan: Last Stand on Ord Mantell 1|''Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan: Last Stand on Ord Mantell'' 1]], established in its introduction to take place five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to {{Film|I}}, which is dated by ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], five years after [[37 BBY/Legends|37 BBY]]. {{Quote|Polvin wasn't impervious to the dark side.|Even Piell|<ref name="YT" />}}
'''Polvin Kut''' was a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived nearly two centuries before the onset of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Along with his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]], Kut was called upon to deliver the colonies of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Koba]] from the clutches of an [[Advozse/Legends|Advozse]] [[Warlord/Legends|warmonger]] named [[Tulak]]. Kut's mission was betrayed by [[Spy/Legends|spies]] prior to his arrival, and he fell victim to ambush as soon as he and Yaddle made it to Koba. In the midst of [[Battle of the Koban Gorge|the battle]], Kut gave in to his hatred for Tulak—the man who had [[Death/Legends|murdered]] his family decades earlier—and surrendered himself to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. His judgment impaired, Kut was swiftly overwhelmed by the warlord's forces and killed.

==Biography==
===Mission to Koba===
{{Quote|Over two hundred years ago, the Jedi Knight, Polvin Kut was summoned to liberate the colonies from the Advozse warlock, Tulak.|Even Piell|<ref name="YT" />}}
Polvin Kut was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] who lived more than 200 years before the onset of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="YT" /> Kut was the Master of [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]], a female member of a [[Yoda's species/Legends|rare]] and long-lived [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="YDB" /> Kut instilled in his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] lessons that would stick with her for many years.<ref name="YT" />

[[File:PolvinKutDeath.jpg|thumb|left|Polvin Kut is killed by Advozsec warriors.]]
Polvin Kut's family was [[Death/Legends|murdered]] by an [[Advozse/Legends|Advozse]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] named [[Tulak]]. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] spent decades chasing him, allowing the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] to slowly fuel his desire for revenge. Kut eventually tracked Tulak to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Koba]], where the warlord had [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] the native [[Koban|people]]. Tulak's oppression forced the dissident colonists to send a request to Kut for assistance. However, several of the Jedi's contacts were actually [[Spy/Legends|spies]] for Tulak, and they passed knowledge of Kut's proposed intervention to their leader, who planned to lure Kut into a trap.<ref name="YT" />

Kut brought Yaddle with him to Koba, where, upon their arrival, they were [[Battle of the Koban Gorge|ambushed]] by Advozsec forces led by Tulak himself. Sight of the warlord immediately stimulated Kut's hatred for his family's killer, and the Jedi Master embraced the dark side, which in turn corrupted his judgment with a desperate need for vengeance. Though he and Yaddle fought against insurmountable odds, Polvin Kut was swiftly cut down on a [[bridge]] over the [[Koban Gorge]] by Tulak's warriors. Yaddle was immediately taken into custody by Tulak, who [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] her in a pit after intense [[Torture/Legends|torture]] and interrogation.<ref name="YT" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Even the monument bridge where Polvin Kut lost his life was reconstructed and named for Yaddle's Jedi Master.|Even Piell|<ref name="YT" />}}
[[File:PolvinKutMemorialBridge.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Polvin Kut's memorial bridge]]
Polvin Kut's death left Yaddle without a Master, and her [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]] thus incomplete. In her years of solitude as Tulak's captive, she relived the teachings of her late Master, through which she manage to train herself into [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knighthood]]. Tulak eventually abandoned Koba and, after more than a century of incarceration, Yaddle was freed. She stayed on Koba to help the natives rebuild the planet that Kut had originally come to save. With Yaddle's help, Koban society was nursed back into prosperity. The Kobans also restored the bridge where Kut had fallen and christened it a [[Polvin Kut memorial bridge|monument in his honor]], one complete with support columns fashioned in the Jedi Master's likeness. However, it was destroyed yet again when [[Kalut]], the son of Polvin's nemesis, arrived to [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorize]] the Koba natives in his father's name. Yaddle subsequently defeated Kalut in combat, and his death effectively eliminated generations of Advozse-imposed tyranny, thus completing her Master's work.<ref name="YT" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Revenge clouded his judgment—his reactions—and he paid the price that day in the Koban Gorge.|Even Piell|<ref name="YT" />}}
[[File:YaddlePolvinKoba.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Polvin and Yaddle in battle on Koba]]
Polvin Kut was a fair-skinned man with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and brown hair that he wore braided and bound in a topknot with the braids hanging past his shoulders. He clad himself in traditional [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] and wielded an [[Color/Legends|orange]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] in combat. Kut was a Jedi Master who readily raised his weapon in battle for those who suffered under the heel of Tulak on Koba. However, the murder of his family at Tulak's behest began a slow descent to the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for Kut. He was eventually consumed by his hatred for the warlord, and ultimately died as a result. Prior to his death, Kut taught his Padawan, Yaddle, lessons that remained with her for years and formed the foundation by which she was able to successfully train herself in the Jedi arts. The citizens of Koba recognized Kut for his valor posthumously and immortalized him with the reconstruction of the bridge on which he had fallen.<ref name="YT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Polvin Kut first appeared in author [[Dean Motter]]'s "[[Yaddle's Tale: The One Below]]," which was featured in the [[Star Wars Tales 5|fifth issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Tales]]''. It was later compiled into a [[Star Wars comic books|trade paperback]] version, ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 2]]''. Plausible stories in [[Star Wars Tales 1|''Star Wars Tales'' 1]]–''[[Star Wars Tales 20|20]]'' were initially considered [[Canon|S-canon]] until referenced in a non-''Tales'' source, at which point the subject matter was elevated to C-canon.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM}}</ref> Following the release of ''Star Wars Tales 5'' in the year [[2000]], Kut received mention in [[2002]]'s ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'', thus raising his status to C-canon.<ref name="POTJ">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> He was subsequently mentioned in [[StarWars.com]]'s [[Databank (original)|Databank]], ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 105]]'', and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', all under entries concerning his Padawan, Yaddle.<ref name="YDB" /><ref name="FF105">{{FactFile|105|YAD 1-2|Yaddle}}</ref><ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>
